commit.message:
Add missing Swagger docs for chat, feedback, and conversation endpoints (#1072)

The Swagger UI was missing documentation for the core chat flow
endpoints — the SSE connection, message sending, stream stop, and status
endpoints — plus feedback and conversation management routes.

## Changes

### `server/routes/chat/sessionRoutes.js`
- Added reusable schema components: `ChatMessage`, `ChatRequest`,
`ChatStreamingResponse`, `ChatErrorResponse`
- Documented all four previously undocumented endpoints:
- `GET /apps/{appId}/chat/{chatId}` — SSE event-stream connection
(clients connect here first)
- `POST /apps/{appId}/chat/{chatId}` — send a chat message; streams via
SSE if connected, otherwise returns directly
- `POST /apps/{appId}/chat/{chatId}/stop` — abort the active LLM request
and close the stream
- `GET /apps/{appId}/chat/{chatId}/status` — connection/processing
status

### `server/routes/chat/feedbackRoutes.js`
- Documented `POST /feedback` with full request body schema (rating in
0.5 increments, optional comment, etc.)

### `server/routes/chat/conversationRoutes.js`
- Replaced plain JSDoc comments with `@swagger` annotations for all
three conversation endpoints:
  - `GET /apps/{appId}/conversations/{conversationId}/messages`
- `DELETE
/apps/{appId}/conversations/{conversationId}/messages/{messageId}`
  - `DELETE /apps/{appId}/conversations/{conversationId}`

The Normal API spec now exposes 19 documented paths (up from 11).

commit.message:
Update vite.config.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Sprint 16: FieldId DU, generic Focus.tabOrder, benchmark CI, undo algebraic laws

- Add [<Struct>] FieldId DU and FieldId module (create/value) to Widgets.fs
- All Form and TextForm APIs now use FieldId instead of string for type safety
- Focus.tabOrder made generic ('a list -> 'a -> FocusDirection -> 'a)
- TFFieldErrors and TFSetFieldErrors use Map<FieldId,string>
- Obsolete attributes on Form.fieldFromKey/handleKey now include 'Will be removed in v1.0.'
- README test count corrected: 1,112 -> 1,526
- Benchmark regression CI job added to .github/workflows/ci.yml
  (benchmark-action/github-action-benchmark, 500% alert threshold, fail-on-alert)
- TextEditorTests: added custom FsCheck Arbitraries (SingleLineContent, ContentMsg)
- TextEditorTests: 4 algebraic law property tests for undo/redo subsystem
  Law1: undo(edit(x)).content = x.content (left inverse)
  Law2: undo at empty history is identity (idempotent)
  Law3: redo(undo(edit(x))).content = edit(x).content (right inverse)
  Law4: history depth never exceeds updateWithUndoDepth cap (bounded)
- All 1,530 tests passing (was 1,526; +4 new law tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: combo tracking system with EventBus signals

Add combo_tracker.gd that counts consecutive hits per fighter,
resets on opponent recovery/block/timeout, and emits combo_updated
and hit_confirmed signals through EventBus. Wired into fight_scene.gd
as a child node registered with both fighters.

Closes #68

fix(desktop): use explicit period flag

Harden the desktop-to-Go period handoff by sending tray period selections through a named --period argument instead of relying on positional numeric args. Add Go and Rust regression coverage for named period handling, precedence, and desktop sidecar argument generation.

commit.message:
v0.1.60: OSM voxel carving — tunnels, aeroways, building rotation, server DHT fixes

- terrain.rs: tunnel roads now carve underground AIR passages (5-voxel clearance)
  with ASPHALT floor instead of being skipped; surface terrain unchanged above
- terrain.rs: aeroway carving pass — runways (45m CONCRETE), taxiways (23m),
  aprons/helipads from OsmAeroway ways; surface flattened to grade
- terrain.rs: bridges still elevated in render pipeline (unchanged)
- world_inference.rs: building rotation computed from longest polygon edge angle
  (rotation_y = atan2 of longest edge direction) instead of always 0.0
- server: OSM DHT announce now reads from TileStore (iter_osm_coords) instead
  of scanning legacy flat .bin files
- server: cleanup_old_srtm_dir now called at startup alongside cleanup_old_tile_dir
  to remove old flat elevation files after TileStore migration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* T-SRAF-QA-001: RED gate tests for useTriggerAIParsing session refresh fix

Wave: wave-session-refresh-auth-fix
Task: T-SRAF-QA-001 (qa-builder)
IAA Pre-Brief: IAA-PREBRIEF-WAVE-SESSION-REFRESH-AUTH-FIX-20260309

Adds 4 RED gate tests in:
  modules/mat/tests/wave-session-refresh-auth-fix/wave-sraf-session-refresh.test.ts

Tests (all RED against current implementation, GREEN after T-SRAF-API-001):

T-SRAF-001: supabase.auth.getSession() called AND appears before
            supabase.functions.invoke() in mutationFn (execution order)

            'Authentication required. Please sign in again.' thrown

T-SRAF-003: sessionError identifier present, combined conditional

T-SRAF-004: Auth guard throw appears at lower index than functions.invoke —
            structural guarantee that invoke cannot be reached when session absent

Test approach: file-based source analysis (matches codebase convention).
No live Supabase env required. CI-safe.

Confirmed RED: all 4 tests fail against current code with descriptive
[RED — EXPECTED before T-SRAF-API-001] failure messages.

* T-SRAF-API-001: add session refresh guard in useTriggerAIParsing

Calls supabase.auth.getSession() before supabase.functions.invoke() to
ensure the Authorization header is valid. Throws 'Authentication required.
Please sign in again.' when session is null or getSession returns an error.

Fixes: T-SRAF-001, T-SRAF-002, T-SRAF-003, T-SRAF-004

commit.message:
tools: add milestone report generator for wiki/devblog automation

Adds generate-milestone-report.py that collects merged PRs, closed issues,
commits, and contributors from git/gh CLI to produce:
- Dev Diary markdown draft (for GitHub Discussions)
- Wiki update checklist (suggests which pages need edits based on changed files)

Usage:
  python tools/generate-milestone-report.py --milestone M2 --since 2026-03-01
  python tools/generate-milestone-report.py --milestone M3 --since-tag v0.2.0

commit.message:
fix(ci): harden CI/CD workflows with security fixes and reliability improvements (#222)

* Initial plan

* fix: add pull-requests write permissions to GitHub Actions workflows

Co-authored-by: magyargergo <11230420+magyargergo@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): remove ineffective job-level permissions from reusable workflow

* fix(ci): pass PR write permission from caller to reusable unit-tests workflow

* fix(ci): harden CI/CD workflows with security fixes and reliability improvements

- Pin all actions to commit SHAs to prevent supply-chain attacks
- Fix shell injection in ci-integration.yml by using env vars instead of direct interpolation
- Scope permissions per-job in publish.yml (was granting pull-requests:write to publish job)
- Restrict claude-code-review to trusted contributors only (OWNER/MEMBER/COLLABORATOR)
- Switch claude-code-review to pull_request_target for fork PR support
- Fix fail-fast: false in ci-unit-tests.yml cross-platform matrix
- Remove duplicate ubuntu-latest from unit test matrix
- Add timeouts to all workflow jobs
- Improve kuzu-db test loop to continue on failure and report per-file errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): read thresholds from `vitest.config.ts`

---------

feat: exhaustive deep AC — 1397 entities tested individually

- agents-exhaustive:    207 agents × 5 checks (tools,skills,bench,identity,rank)
- teams-exhaustive:      33 teams × 4 checks  (members,agents-exist,bench,desc)
- skills-exhaustive:   1082 skills × 3 checks  (length,frontmatter,no-bad-vars)

All deterministic, no LLM calls, runs in <3 seconds.
run_deep_bench(exhaustive=True) includes all 1397 + 53 infra cases.

Results: workflows 51/51, patterns 24/24 (100%), agents 146/207,
teams 23/33, skills 943/1082 — reveals real config gaps to fix.

feat: add Ed25519 prekey signature cryptographic verification

- Add verify_prekey_signature() helper in key-service using ed25519-dalek
- upload_prekeys(): fetch device verifying_key, verify each Kyber OTPK signature
  (prologue: KonstruktX3DH-v1 || 0x00 0x10 || public_key)
- upload_kyber_signed_prekey(): same verification for Kyber SPK
- register_device() in auth-service: verify classic signed_prekey_signature
  (prologue: KonstruktX3DH-v1 || 0x00 0x01 || signed_prekey_public)

Server now rejects keys with invalid Ed25519 signatures, activating the
domain-separation and downgrade-protection guarantees of the KonstruktX3DH-v1
prologue scheme.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Build complete Employee Management System (Node.js + React)

- Backend: Express + better-sqlite3 REST API on port 3001
  - CRUD endpoints for employees (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
  - Department filtering via query param
  - Input validation and unique email constraint handling
  - Rate limiting (100 req/15min) via express-rate-limit
  - CORS support

- Frontend: Create React App with component architecture
  - EmployeeTable: displays all employees with edit/delete actions
  - EmployeeForm: modal form for add/edit with validation
  - DepartmentFilter: dropdown to filter by department
  - employeeApi: fetch-based API client
  - Dev proxy configured to http://localhost:3001

- .gitignore: excludes node_modules, build artifacts, SQLite DB files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: 彻底删除 _LOOKBOOK_KEYWORDS 硬编码 — lookbook 意图由 LLM 语义理解判断

根因：关键词匹配无法区分语义意图（"保持一致性"≠lookbook，"一组照片"≠lookbook）
每删一个关键词就会有下一个误命中，这是打地鼠的游戏。

治本方案：
1. 删除 _LOOKBOOK_KEYWORDS 和 _is_lookbook 硬编码标志
2. 多输出场景同时给 LLM 三个工具：generate_ai_content + propose_plan + analyze_for_lookbook
3. LLM 根据语义理解自己决定走编辑+plan 还是 lookbook 分析
4. lookbook 提示模块通过 _check_lookbook 语义检查触发（历史 plan、"N组/N套"、"多角度"）
5. 删除 _build_context_sections / _build_llm_messages 中的 is_lookbook 参数
6. _select_prompt_modules 不再用 override_lookbook，改用 _check_lookbook

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Position-based NFA reduction for bitnfa backend (#16)

* Initial plan

* Implement position-based NFA reduction for bitnfa backend

Reduce bit-NFA positions by eliminating epsilon-only intermediate states
from the Thompson NFA (Glushkov-equivalent construction). Only states
with outgoing byte transitions plus the accept state are assigned bit
positions. Includes forward and backward reachability pruning.

Typical position reduction: 25-58% fewer positions vs raw Thompson NFA.

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for position-NFA reduction optimization

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix shallow list copy, add alternation uint32 tests

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Reusable agent skill for testing a target (demo, script, pipeline,
CLI command) without modifying source code, then writing a structured
log of the process, outcomes, anomalies, and issues.

  identify anomalies, write log, no code changes)
- agents/openai.yaml: explicit-invocation-only (allow_implicit_invocation: false)
- references/log-template.md: log structure with per-step result codes
  (pass/fail/anomaly) and anomaly severity classification

commit.message:
fix(polling): close idle sessions when CWD is shared with another session (#30)

Sessions never transitioned to "closed" when another copilot session was
active in the same working directory. The is_session_process_running
check matches by CWD, so any active session in the same repo kept old
sessions appearing as "process running".

Add a staleness check: when a session is idle and its last event is older
than 5 minutes, close it regardless of process_running — the running
process likely belongs to a different session sharing the same CWD.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ui): persist window size and position with tauri-plugin-window-state (#28)

Replace custom screen_width setting with tauri-plugin-window-state plugin
that automatically saves and restores window size, position, maximized,
and fullscreen state.

- Add tauri-plugin-window-state dependency
- Register plugin with SIZE, POSITION, MAXIMIZED, FULLSCREEN flags
- Save window state on close (before hiding to tray)
- Remove screen_width from Settings struct, DB layer, and save command
- Remove Screen Width slider from Settings UI

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: CombatEngine momentum increment + threshold effects (#1274)

Implements WI-C (momentum increment) and WI-D (threshold effects) for all 4 momentum classes.

- Warrior Fury (max 5): Add(1) on attack dealt + hit taken; Consume() → 2× damage
- Mage Arcane Charge (max 3): Add(1) after ability; Consume() → 0 mana + 1.25× damage
- Paladin Devotion (max 4): Add(1) on DivineShield absorb/cast + LayOnHands; Consume() → Stun on HolyStrike  
- Ranger Focus (max 3): Add(1) on 0-damage enemy turn; Reset on HP damage; Consume() → effectiveDef=0
- Momentum reset on flee

InitPlayerMomentum() called at combat start. AddRangerFocusIfNoDamage() helper at 5 call sites.

Rebased cleanly onto master after #1293 merge. Build: 0 errors, 0 warnings.

Closes part of #1274

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: MomentumResource model + display bar (#1274)

Implements MomentumResource model with Current, Maximum, IsCharged, Add(), Reset(), and Consume() (atomic check+reset).

Adds Player.Momentum property (nullable, wired for Warrior/Mage/Paladin/Ranger).
Adds momentum bar to stats panel display with class-specific labels.

Fix applied during review: added Consume() method (required by CombatEngine and test suite).

Closes part of #1274

Co-authored-by: foreleven <4785594+foreleven@users.noreply.github.com>

v0.1.59: fix persistent 1fps — lazy collider was extracting mesh for every chunk

The lazy collider section (chunks with mesh but no collider, awaiting player
approach) called extract_chunk_mesh() on *every* un-collidered chunk per frame
just to compute a center point for distance checking. With 48 un-collidered
chunks × ~80ms per extract = ~3.8 seconds per frame = persistent 1fps even
after loading completed.

Fix: compute chunk center from voxel coordinate arithmetic (O(1), no I/O) for
the distance check. Only call extract_chunk_mesh() once when a chunk is actually
within COLLIDER_RANGE_M and needs its collider built.

Verified: 1fps → 60fps stable in headless 60s test.
  Before: FPS: 1 (entire session)
  After:  FPS: 1 (load) → 19-22 (first collider batch) → 60 (steady state)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve ONNX export workflow failure — protobuf 2GB limit and constant baking (#6)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve ONNX export workflow failure with protobuf size limit and dynamic dimension patches

Key changes:
1. Use file-path based onnx.checker.check_model() to avoid the 2GB protobuf serialization limit
2. Apply monkey-patches to ml-sharp's initializer to keep dynamic dimension (H/W) computations
   in the PyTorch tensor graph instead of extracting as Python scalars
3. Disable constant folding to prevent baking large intermediate tensors as ONNX constants
4. Gracefully handle onnxsim simplification failures for large models
5. Update workflow to handle external data files for models > 2GB

Co-authored-by: KotoriK <52659125+KotoriK@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: address code review - cleaner exception handling and explicit conditional git add

Co-authored-by: KotoriK <52659125+KotoriK@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: use save_as_external_data=True to split ONNX model weights into sidecar file

After torch.onnx.export(), the model is re-saved with
save_as_external_data=True, moving tensor initializers into a
companion .onnx.data file. This keeps the .onnx protobuf well under
the 2GB limit.

On the web side, onnxInference.ts now probes for the .onnx.data
sidecar and passes it via the externalData session option.

Co-authored-by: KotoriK <52659125+KotoriK@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review — spelling consistency and threshold comment

Co-authored-by: KotoriK <52659125+KotoriK@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message: Fix Telegram Markdown parse errors and MarketDataStore thread-safety (#65) * Initial plan * fix: Telegram parse_mode errors and thread-safety in MarketDataStore - _broadcast_close_raw(): remove parse_mode="Markdown" (plain text) - _broadcast_close(): switch to parse_mode="HTML" - _format_close_message(): convert *..* to .., remove # from symbol - format_alert(): remove # prefix from symbol name - MarketDataStore._lock: Lock -> RLock for re-entrancy safety - _ensure_buffers(): use setdefault() to avoid dict mutation race Co-authored-by: kishore446 <78694720+kishore446@users.noreply.github.com> --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: kishore446 <78694720+kishore446@users.noreply.github.com>

Sprint 15.1: fix all expert panel P1/P2/P3 issues

P1-A: fieldFromKey bridge now matches KeyPressed(k, Modifiers.None)
       — silently ignored Ctrl/Shift modifier in key-only legacy handler

P1-B: Remove dead code block in applyToFocused
       — clearedServerErrors/newStatus were computed inside row-mapping
         lambda and discarded; actual status update lives in second pass

P1-C: Add TFFieldErrors per-field clearing tests (the feature's core
       purpose was previously untested)
       — 'editing focused field removes its error from TFFieldErrors status'
       — 'editing last errored field transitions status to TFEditing'

P2-A: Add searchAsyncFromModel test in FuzzyFinderTests
       — verifies Candidates cache is used, toString not called again

P2-B: Property tests now cover multi-line content
       — removed .Replace('\n', ' ') stripping; col is computed from
         m0.Lines.[0].Length so it is valid for any input

P2-C: Convert fun ()->  testProperty to plain test
       — renamed to 'TESelectAll+move do not create undo history'

P2-D: Add El.fgOpt and El.bgOpt to Element.fs as module-level combinators
       — ProgressBar.view now uses El.fgOpt instead of inline withFg

P3-A: Rename FormField.Key -> FormField.Id
       — avoids shadowing the Key DU type; consistent with TextFormField.Id

P3-B: Fix SplitPaneModel doc comment ('Mutable state' -> immutable record)

P3-C: Add SplitPane snapshot tests (horizontal/vertical/grown)
       — 3 new verified snapshots

P3-D: Document grow/shrink negative-step behavior in XML doc comments

1,524 tests passing (was 1,518)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(vs-extension): Sprint 6 — MEF smoke tests, RunTestAtCursor, connection status

P0: MefAttributeTests.cs — 12 reflection-only MEF attribute tests, permanent regression
guard for ContentType attributes on all 4 MEF exports. 51 tests total, all passing.
Suppresses benign NU1603 in test project csproj.

P2: RunTestAtCursorCommand.cs — Shift+Alt+T. Uses Seemann algorithm (last test with
Line <= cursorLine). Runs via RunTestsAsync(FullName). SSE delivers results to inline
adornments and glyph margin automatically.

P3: ConnectionStatus in LiveTestingData — flips from connecting to connected on first
SSE StateChanged. Shown in Live Testing Dashboard toolbar.

String resource: SageFs.RunTestAtCursor.DisplayName
InlineEvalResultTracker.cs: three-state tracker prep for future inline eval adornments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
